title: Monotonic-constraint expert on physically directional features
summary: Several core signals should be directionally stable in aggregate: more laps since last stop, more wear relative to expected life, more laps beyond finish window, and stronger stop-evidence intensity should not usually reduce pit risk all else equal.
rationale: Several core signals should be directionally stable in aggregate: more laps since last stop, more wear relative to expected life, more laps beyond finish window, and stronger stop-evidence intensity should not usually reduce pit risk all else equal. Monotonic constraints are documented in both XGBoost and CatBoost and can suppress fold-specific inversions from noisy handcrafted interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a second expert with monotonic constraints on a small trusted subset of features such as `TyreLife_ExpectedLife_Ratio`, `Compound_LapsBeyondFinishWindow`, `LapsRemaining_AfterExpectedLife`, `PitReadiness_CooldownAdjusted`, and maybe decreasing constraints on `PitCooldown_Suppression` or very-short remaining-laps variables where appropriate. Blend this constrained expert with the unconstrained baseline instead of forcing all features into constraints.
expected_effect: Lower overfitting and more stable LB transfer by preserving the physically sensible shape of the strongest readiness signals.
risk: Badly chosen constraints can hurt because F1 strategy has real exceptions, especially wet races, safety-car-like latent regimes, and endgame no-stop situations; keep the constrained feature set small.
